<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Computational number theory</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false,
      macros:{
        '\\Spec':'\\Spec',
        '\\Gal':'\\Gal',
        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',
        '\\tr':'\\tr',
        '\\ad':'\\ad',
        '\\ind':'\\ind'
      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

← Notebook
1 Primality 2 Factoring 3 LLL 4 Modular arithmetic 5 Schoof 6 Class groups

Computational number theory

Algorithms that turn the structural results of number theory into running code — primality, factoring, lattice reduction, point counting, and class-group computation, with their complexity classes attached.

blocks.html:
1. Primality testing

How do you decide whether a 1024-bit integer is prime? Trial division costs $O(\sqrt n)$ — exponential in $\log n$ and hopeless. The path forward is Fermat's little theorem: if $p$ is prime and $\gcd(a,p)=1$, then $a^{p-1}\equiv 1\pmod p$. Compute $a^{n-1}\bmod n$ by fast exponentiation in $O(\log n)$ multiplies; if it isn't $1$, then $n$ is composite — and you've produced a witness.

The trouble: Carmichael numbers like $561 = 3\cdot 11\cdot 17$ pass Fermat for every base coprime to $n$. Miller–Rabin patches the leak. Write $n-1 = 2^s \cdot d$ with $d$ odd. If $n$ is prime, then for any $a$ coprime to $n$, either $a^d\equiv 1$ or $a^{2^r d}\equiv -1$ for some $0\le r

Miller–Rabin witness search
Pick $n$, pick a base $a$, watch the squaring chain.
Click Run.

For deterministic guarantees, AKS (Agrawal–Kayal–Saxena, 2002) tests whether $(x+a)^n \equiv x^n + a \pmod{n,\ x^r - 1}$ for sufficiently many $a$ and a carefully chosen small $r$. It runs in $O((\log n)^{6+\epsilon})$ — polynomial, deterministic, unconditional. In practice Miller–Rabin remains the workhorse; AKS is theoretical bedrock.

See also
  • Time complexity and big-O
  • Modular arithmetic and Euler's theorem

Used in
Computational number theory · Integer factoring
</section>

2. Integer factoring

Primality is in $P$. Factoring is conjecturally not. The fastest known classical algorithm — the general number-field sieve — runs in subexponential time $L_n[1/3, (64/9)^{1/3}]$. RSA's security rests on this gap.

Pollard's $\rho$ is the entry-level method: iterate $x_{i+1} = x_i^2 + c \bmod n$ starting from $x_0=2$. Reduced modulo a prime divisor $p$ of $n$, the sequence enters a cycle in $O(\sqrt p)$ steps by the birthday paradox. Floyd's tortoise–hare detects the cycle without storing the whole sequence, and $\gcd(x_i - x_j, n)$ extracts a nontrivial factor.

Pollard $\rho$ — the cycle
Watch $x \mapsto x^2+1 \bmod n$ enter its $\rho$-shaped orbit.
Click Trace.

For RSA-sized targets one needs Pollard's $p-1$ (works when $p-1$ is smooth), the quadratic sieve (subexp $L_n[1/2, 1]$, good up to ~120 digits), and finally the number-field sieve. All three use the same recipe: collect relations of the form "smooth product equals smooth product modulo $n$" in a factor base, find a $\mathbb F_2$-linear dependence, extract a square root, take a $\gcd$.

$L_n[\alpha, c] = \exp\!\Big(c\,(\log n)^\alpha (\log\log n)^{1-\alpha}\Big)$ interpolates between polynomial ($\alpha=0$) and exponential ($\alpha=1$). The factoring records sit at $\alpha = 1/3$.

See also
  • P and NP
  • RSA
</section>

3. LLL — short bases in polynomial time

A lattice $L\subset\mathbb R^n$ is the $\mathbb Z$-span of $n$ linearly independent vectors $b_1,\dots,b_n$. The shortest-vector problem (SVP) is NP-hard under randomized reductions. Lenstra–Lenstra–Lovász (1982) finds, in polynomial time, a basis whose first vector is at most $2^{(n-1)/2}$ times the true shortest — exponentially loose, yet enough to break a remarkable amount of cryptography.

The algorithm alternates two moves on the Gram–Schmidt orthogonalization $b_i^*$:

  • Size reduction: replace $b_i$ by $b_i - \lfloor \mu_{ij}\rceil b_j$ for $j
  • Lovász swap: if $\|b_{i}^*\|^2 < (\delta - \mu_{i,i-1}^2)\|b_{i-1}^*\|^2$ for some $\delta\in(1/4, 1)$, swap $b_{i-1}$ and $b_i$.
LLL on a 2D lattice
Drag the slider — apply size reduction and swap repeatedly until stable.
Initial basis loaded.

Coppersmith's theorem. Given a polynomial $f(x)\in\mathbb Z[x]$ of degree $d$ and a modulus $N$, all integer roots of $f \bmod N$ with $|x|

LLL also accelerates: integer-relation detection (PSLQ), knapsack cryptanalysis, sub-routines inside Buchmann's class-group algorithm (§6), and post-quantum lattice cryptosystems whose hardness assumptions sit one notch above LLL's reach.

See also
  • Lattice-based cryptography — the post-quantum schemes whose security rests on harder lattice problems beyond LLL.

See also
  • Lattice-based cryptography — the post-quantum schemes whose security rests on harder lattice problems beyond LLL.
  • Time complexity and big-O
  • Lattice-based and post-quantum cryptography

Used in
Computational number theory · Class & unit group computation
</section>

4. Modular arithmetic algorithms

Every higher-level routine in this notebook calls a handful of primitives. They look elementary; making them fast is what enables 2048-bit RSA and 256-bit ECC.

Fast modular exponentiation

Compute $a^k \bmod n$ in $O(\log k)$ multiplies by repeated squaring. Read the binary expansion $k = \sum k_i 2^i$ from the top down; at each step square, and multiply by $a$ when $k_i=1$.

Square-and-multiply
Trace $7^{13} \bmod 11$ — every bit costs one squaring plus an optional multiply.
Click Trace.

Tonelli–Shanks: square roots mod $p$

Given $a$ with $\left(\tfrac{a}{p}\right) = 1$, find $r$ with $r^2 \equiv a \pmod p$. The easy case $p\equiv 3\pmod 4$: take $r = a^{(p+1)/4}$, since $r^2 = a \cdot a^{(p-1)/2} \equiv a$. For $p\equiv 1\pmod 4$, factor $p-1 = 2^s Q$ with $Q$ odd, pick a non-residue $z$, and chase $r$ down through powers of a $2^s$-th root of unity.

Chinese remainder theorem

To reconstruct $x$ from $x \bmod m_1, \dots, x \bmod m_k$ for pairwise coprime $m_i$, Garner's algorithm runs in $O(k^2)$ multiplies in the small moduli — far faster than working modulo the full product. CRT splits modular exponentiation in RSA: compute $a^d \bmod p$ and $a^d \bmod q$, then glue. This is a 4× speedup in practice.

See also
  • Modular arithmetic and Euler's theorem

Used in
Computational number theory · Schoof & point counting
</section>

5. Schoof's algorithm — counting points on elliptic curves

Given $E/\mathbb F_p$, Hasse's bound says $\#E(\mathbb F_p) = p+1-t$ with $|t|\le 2\sqrt p$. Brute-force counting is $O(p)$ — exponential in $\log p$. Schoof's algorithm (1985) computes $t$ in time polynomial in $\log p$, and was the first deep result of the kind. It is the algorithmic engine behind every standardized elliptic curve.

The trick: determine $t \bmod \ell$ for many small primes $\ell$, then reconstruct $t$ by CRT once $\prod \ell > 4\sqrt p$. To find $t \bmod \ell$, work on the $\ell$-torsion subgroup $E[\ell] \cong (\mathbb Z/\ell)^2$, represented by the $\ell$-th division polynomial $\psi_\ell(x)$. Frobenius $\phi_p(x,y) = (x^p, y^p)$ satisfies its characteristic equation

$\phi_p^2 - t\,\phi_p + p \equiv 0 \quad \text{on } E[\ell].$

For each candidate $t' \in \{0,1,\dots,\ell-1\}$, test whether the equation holds in $\mathbb F_p[x,y] / (\psi_\ell, E)$; the unique $t'$ that works is $t \bmod \ell$.

Schoof — CRT recovery of $t$
Toggle which small primes $\ell$ are used; watch the Hasse window collapse onto $t$.
Each $\ell$ pins $t$ to a residue class.

SEA (Schoof–Elkies–Atkin) speeds Schoof up by a factor of about $\ell$ when $\ell$ is an Elkies prime — when the modular polynomial $\Phi_\ell(j(E), Y)$ has a root in $\mathbb F_p$, meaning Frobenius has rational eigenvalues on $E[\ell]$. SEA is the algorithm that ECDSA-2.0 implementations actually run.
</section>

6. Computing class groups & units

For a number field $K = \mathbb Q(\alpha)$ with ring of integers $\mathcal O_K$, two finiteness theorems govern the arithmetic:

  • The class group $\mathrm{Cl}(K) = \mathcal I(\mathcal O_K) / P(\mathcal O_K)$ is finite — its order $h_K$ is the class number.
  • The unit group $\mathcal O_K^\times \cong \mu(K) \oplus \mathbb Z^{r_1+r_2-1}$ is finitely generated (Dirichlet); the regulator $R_K$ is the covolume of the log-embedding lattice.

Buchmann's algorithm (1990) computes $h_K$, $R_K$, and a system of fundamental units in subexponential time — provided the generalized Riemann hypothesis for Hecke $L$-functions of $K$ holds. The recipe:

  1. Fix a factor base. Take all prime ideals $\mathfrak p$ of $\mathcal O_K$ with norm below a bound $B \approx (\log|\Delta_K|)^2$. Under GRH, these generate $\mathrm{Cl}(K)$.
  2. Collect relations. Random elements $\alpha\in\mathcal O_K$ whose principal ideal $(\alpha)$ factors over the factor base give a relation vector in $\mathbb Z^{|S|}$, plus a logarithm vector recording $\log|\sigma_i(\alpha)|$ for each archimedean place.
  3. Reduce the lattice. Apply LLL to the combined relation/log lattice; its Smith normal form yields the class-group invariants and a basis of fundamental units.

The classical small example: $K = \mathbb Q(\sqrt{-5})$, $\mathcal O_K = \mathbb Z[\sqrt{-5}]$. The famous non-unique factorization $6 = 2\cdot 3 = (1+\sqrt{-5})(1-\sqrt{-5})$ is a relation in the factor base $S = \{\mathfrak p_2, \mathfrak p_3, \mathfrak p_3'\}$; after reduction the class group is $\mathbb Z/2\mathbb Z$, $h=2$.

Buchmann — relation matrix to Smith normal form
Toggle relations on a small factor base for $\mathbb Q(\sqrt{-5})$; the SNF reveals the class group.
Empty matrix — no relations yet.
See also
  • Prime-ideal factorization in $\mathcal O_K$ — the structural prerequisite Buchmann's factor base relies on.
  • Class group & units — the finiteness theorems being computed.

See also
  • Prime-ideal factorization in $\mathcal O_K$ — the structural prerequisite Buchmann's factor base relies on.
  • Class group & units — the finiteness theorems being computed.
  • Ideal factorization and ramification
  • Class group and units
</section>

Connections

Computational number theory is where structural theorems become engineering. Open frontiers cluster around two questions: can the subexponential factoring barrier be broken classically (currently no candidate algorithm), and can post-quantum lattice schemes survive cryptanalytic improvements that chip away at LLL's gap to exact SVP?

  • RSA — depends entirely on the conjectured hardness of factoring.
  • ECC — needs Schoof–SEA point counting to certify curve order.
  • Algebraic number theory — class numbers and regulators above small discriminant are computed, not proved by hand.
  • Complexity theory — primality is in $P$, factoring conjecturally is not; the gap is what cryptography monetizes.
  • $p$-adic methods — Hensel lifting underlies many efficient root-finding routines used inside these algorithms.

<script>
/* ==== Miller–Rabin witness search ==== */
(function(){
  const nIn = $("#mr-n"), aIn = $("#mr-a"), out = $("#mr-out"), svg = $("#mr-svg");
  const goBtn = $("#mr-go"), randBtn = $("#mr-rand");
  if(!svg) return;

  function modpow(a, e, n){
    a = ((a % n) + n) % n;
    let r = 1n; let base = BigInt(a); let exp = BigInt(e); const N = BigInt(n);
    while(exp > 0n){
      if(exp & 1n) r = (r * base) % N;
      base = (base * base) % N;
      exp >>= 1n;
    }
    return Number(r);
  }
  function gcd(a,b){ while(b){[a,b]=[b,a%b];} return a; }

  function run(){
    const n = parseInt(nIn.value, 10);
    const a = parseInt(aIn.value, 10);
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    if(n < 3 || n % 2 === 0){
      out.textContent = "n must be an odd integer ≥ 3.";
      return;
    }
    if(a < 2 || a >= n){
      out.textContent = "Pick base a with 2 ≤ a < n.";
      return;
    }
    if(gcd(a, n) !== 1){
      out.textContent = `gcd(${a},${n}) = ${gcd(a,n)} — composite witness by GCD alone.`;
      return;
    }
    let d = n - 1, s = 0;
    while(d % 2 === 0){ d /= 2; s += 1; }
    const chain = [];
    let x = modpow(a, d, n);
    chain.push({label: `a^d = ${a}^${d}`, value: x});
    let verdict = null;
    if(x === 1 || x === n - 1){ verdict = "probable prime"; }
    for(let r = 0; r < s - 1 && verdict === null; r++){
      x = modpow(x, 2, n);
      chain.push({label: `square → r=${r+1}`, value: x});
      if(x === n - 1){ verdict = "probable prime"; break; }
      if(x === 1){ verdict = "composite (nontrivial √1)"; break; }
    }
    if(verdict === null) verdict = "composite (no -1 hit)";

    const W = 760, H = 220;
    const stepX = Math.min(120, (W - 80) / Math.max(1, chain.length));
    chain.forEach((c, i) => {
      const x0 = 40 + i * stepX, y0 = 90;
      const isPrime = c.value === 1 || c.value === n - 1;
      const fill = isPrime ? "var(--green)" : (verdict.startsWith("composite") && i === chain.length - 1 ? "var(--pink)" : "var(--panel2)");
      const stroke = isPrime ? "var(--green)" : "var(--blue)";
      svg.appendChild(SVG("rect", {x: x0 - 50, y: y0 - 26, width: 100, height: 52, rx: 8, fill, stroke, "stroke-width": 1.5, "fill-opacity": isPrime ? 0.3 : 1}));
      const t1 = SVG("text", {x: x0, y: y0 - 6, "text-anchor": "middle", "font-size": 12, fill: "var(--mute)"});
      t1.textContent = c.label;
      svg.appendChild(t1);
      const t2 = SVG("text", {x: x0, y: y0 + 14, "text-anchor": "middle", "font-size": 14, "font-weight": 600, fill: "#fff"});
      t2.textContent = c.value === n - 1 ? `n−1 ≡ −1` : String(c.value);
      svg.appendChild(t2);
      if(i > 0){
        drawArrow(svg, [x0 - stepX + 50, y0], [x0 - 50, y0], {color: "var(--mute)", markerId: "arr-mr", width: 1.2});
      }
    });
    const verdictColor = verdict.startsWith("composite") ? "var(--pink)" : "var(--green)";
    const vt = SVG("text", {x: 380, y: 180, "text-anchor": "middle", "font-size": 16, "font-weight": 600, fill: verdictColor});
    vt.textContent = `n=${n}, base ${a}: ${verdict}`;
    svg.appendChild(vt);
    out.textContent = `n−1 = 2^${s} · ${d}. Chain length ${chain.length}. ${verdict}.`;
  }

  goBtn.addEventListener("click", run);
  randBtn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    const n = parseInt(nIn.value, 10);
    if(n > 3){
      aIn.value = String(2 + Math.floor(Math.random() * (n - 3)));
      run();
    }
  });
  run();
})();

/* ==== Pollard rho ==== */
(function(){
  const nIn = $("#rho-n"), out = $("#rho-out"), svg = $("#rho-svg");
  const goBtn = $("#rho-go"), stepBtn = $("#rho-step"), resetBtn = $("#rho-reset");
  if(!svg) return;

  let state = null;

  function gcd(a,b){ a = Math.abs(a); b = Math.abs(b); while(b){[a,b]=[b,a%b];} return a; }
  function f(x, n){ return ((x * x) + 1) % n; }

  function reset(){
    const n = parseInt(nIn.value, 10);
    state = {n, tortoise: 2, hare: 2, history: [{tortoise: 2, hare: 2}], factor: null};
    redraw();
    out.textContent = `Initialized at x=2 mod ${n}. Press Step or Trace.`;
  }

  function step(){
    if(!state || state.factor) return false;
    const n = state.n;
    state.tortoise = f(state.tortoise, n);
    state.hare = f(f(state.hare, n), n);
    state.history.push({tortoise: state.tortoise, hare: state.hare});
    const d = gcd(Math.abs(state.tortoise - state.hare), n);
    if(d > 1 && d < n){
      state.factor = d;
      out.textContent = `Step ${state.history.length - 1}: tortoise=${state.tortoise}, hare=${state.hare}. gcd(${Math.abs(state.tortoise - state.hare)}, ${n}) = ${d} → factor!`;
      redraw();
      return false;
    }
    if(d === n){
      out.textContent = `Failure at step ${state.history.length - 1}: gcd hit n. Try a different starting point.`;
      state.factor = -1;
      redraw();
      return false;
    }
    out.textContent = `Step ${state.history.length - 1}: tortoise=${state.tortoise}, hare=${state.hare}, gcd=${d}.`;
    redraw();
    return true;
  }

  function redraw(){
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    const W = 760, H = 280;
    const cx = 380, cy = 140, R = 110;
    const n = state.n;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle", {cx, cy, r: R, fill: "none", stroke: "var(--line)", "stroke-dasharray": "3 4"}));
    const tl = SVG("text", {x: cx, y: 25, "text-anchor": "middle", "font-size": 12, fill: "var(--mute)"});
    tl.textContent = `residues mod ${n}`;
    svg.appendChild(tl);

    const pos = v => {
      const theta = (v / n) * Math.PI * 2 - Math.PI / 2;
      return [cx + R * Math.cos(theta), cy + R * Math.sin(theta)];
    };

    state.history.forEach((h, i) => {
      const alpha = 0.2 + 0.6 * (i / Math.max(1, state.history.length - 1));
      if(i > 0){
        const a = pos(state.history[i-1].tortoise);
        const b = pos(h.tortoise);
        svg.appendChild(SVG("line", {x1: a[0], y1: a[1], x2: b[0], y2: b[1], stroke: "var(--cyan)", "stroke-width": 1.2, "stroke-opacity": alpha}));
      }
    });

    const seen = new Set();
    state.history.forEach(h => {
      [h.tortoise, h.hare].forEach(v => {
        if(seen.has(v)) return;
        seen.add(v);
        const [x, y] = pos(v);
        svg.appendChild(SVG("circle", {cx: x, cy: y, r: 3, fill: "var(--mute)"}));
      });
    });

    const [tx, ty] = pos(state.tortoise);
    const [hx, hy] = pos(state.hare);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle", {cx: tx, cy: ty, r: 7, fill: "var(--cyan)", stroke: "#fff", "stroke-width": 1.5}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("circle", {cx: hx, cy: hy, r: 7, fill: "var(--yellow)", stroke: "#fff", "stroke-width": 1.5}));

    const lt = SVG("text", {x: tx, y: ty - 12, "text-anchor": "middle", "font-size": 11, fill: "var(--cyan)"});
    lt.textContent = `🐢 ${state.tortoise}`;
    svg.appendChild(lt);
    const lh = SVG("text", {x: hx, y: hy + 18, "text-anchor": "middle", "font-size": 11, fill: "var(--yellow)"});
    lh.textContent = `🐇 ${state.hare}`;
    svg.appendChild(lh);

    if(state.factor && state.factor > 0){
      const ft = SVG("text", {x: cx, y: cy + 4, "text-anchor": "middle", "font-size": 18, "font-weight": 700, fill: "var(--green)"});
      ft.textContent = `${n} = ${state.factor} · ${n / state.factor}`;
      svg.appendChild(ft);
    }
    const sl = SVG("text", {x: 30, y: H - 10, "font-size": 12, fill: "var(--mute)"});
    sl.textContent = `step ${state.history.length - 1}`;
    svg.appendChild(sl);
  }

  goBtn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    reset();
    let safety = 200;
    while(safety-- > 0 && step()){}
  });
  stepBtn.addEventListener("click", () => { if(!state) reset(); step(); });
  resetBtn.addEventListener("click", reset);
  reset();
})();

/* ==== LLL on a 2D lattice ==== */
(function(){
  const skewIn = $("#lll-skew"), out = $("#lll-out"), svg = $("#lll-svg");
  const stepBtn = $("#lll-step"), fullBtn = $("#lll-full"), resetBtn = $("#lll-reset");
  if(!svg) return;

  let basis = null;

  function dot(u,v){ return u[0]*v[0] + u[1]*v[1]; }
  function norm2(u){ return dot(u,u); }
  function gso(b){
    const b1s = [b[0][0], b[0][1]];
    const mu = dot(b[1], b1s) / norm2(b1s);
    const b2s = [b[1][0] - mu * b1s[0], b[1][1] - mu * b1s[1]];
    return {b1s, b2s, mu};
  }

  function reset(){
    const skew = parseInt(skewIn.value, 10);
    basis = [[5, 1], [skew, 4]];
    redraw("Initial basis loaded.");
  }

  function lllStep(){
    const {b1s, b2s, mu} = gso(basis);
    const m = Math.round(mu);
    if(Math.abs(mu - m) > 0.5){
      basis[1] = [basis[1][0] - m * basis[0][0], basis[1][1] - m * basis[0][1]];
      redraw(`Size reduced: b₂ ← b₂ − ${m}·b₁ (μ was ${mu.toFixed(3)}).`);
      return true;
    }
    const delta = 0.75;
    if(norm2(b2s) < (delta - mu * mu) * norm2(b1s)){
      [basis[0], basis[1]] = [basis[1], basis[0]];
      redraw(`Lovász swap: b₁ ↔ b₂ (was too long).`);
      return true;
    }
    redraw(`LLL-reduced. ‖b₁‖²=${norm2(basis[0]).toFixed(2)}, ‖b₂‖²=${norm2(basis[1]).toFixed(2)}.`);
    return false;
  }

  function redraw(msg){
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    const W = 760, H = 320;
    const cx = W / 2, cy = H / 2;
    const scale = 18;

    for(let i = -20; i <= 20; i++){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line", {x1: cx + i * scale, y1: 0, x2: cx + i * scale, y2: H, stroke: "var(--line)", "stroke-opacity": 0.3}));
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line", {x1: 0, y1: cy + i * scale, x2: W, y2: cy + i * scale, stroke: "var(--line)", "stroke-opacity": 0.3}));
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line", {x1: 0, y1: cy, x2: W, y2: cy, stroke: "var(--mute)", "stroke-opacity": 0.6}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line", {x1: cx, y1: 0, x2: cx, y2: H, stroke: "var(--mute)", "stroke-opacity": 0.6}));

    for(let i = -10; i <= 10; i++){
      for(let j = -10; j <= 10; j++){
        const x = i * basis[0][0] + j * basis[1][0];
        const y = i * basis[0][1] + j * basis[1][1];
        const px = cx + x * scale, py = cy - y * scale;
        if(px > 0 && px < W && py > 0 && py < H){
          svg.appendChild(SVG("circle", {cx: px, cy: py, r: 1.8, fill: "var(--mute)", "fill-opacity": 0.7}));
        }
      }
    }

    const draw = (v, color, label) => {
      const x = cx + v[0] * scale, y = cy - v[1] * scale;
      drawArrow(svg, [cx, cy], [x, y], {color, markerId: `arr-lll-${label}`, width: 2.5});
      const lbl = SVG("text", {x: x + 6, y: y - 4, "font-size": 13, "font-weight": 600, fill: color});
      lbl.textContent = label;
      svg.appendChild(lbl);
    };
    draw(basis[0], "var(--cyan)", "b₁");
    draw(basis[1], "var(--yellow)", "b₂");

    const info = SVG("text", {x: 20, y: 24, "font-size": 12, fill: "var(--mute)"});
    info.textContent = `b₁=(${basis[0][0]},${basis[0][1]})  b₂=(${basis[1][0]},${basis[1][1]})`;
    svg.appendChild(info);

    out.textContent = msg;
  }

  skewIn.addEventListener("input", reset);
  stepBtn.addEventListener("click", () => { if(!basis) reset(); lllStep(); });
  fullBtn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    if(!basis) reset();
    let safety = 50;
    while(safety-- > 0 && lllStep()){}
  });
  resetBtn.addEventListener("click", reset);
  reset();
})();

/* ==== Square-and-multiply ==== */
(function(){
  const aIn = $("#exp-a"), kIn = $("#exp-k"), nIn = $("#exp-n"), out = $("#exp-out"), goBtn = $("#exp-go");
  if(!goBtn) return;
  function run(){
    const a = parseInt(aIn.value, 10), k = parseInt(kIn.value, 10), n = parseInt(nIn.value, 10);
    if(!(a > 0 && k >= 0 && n > 1)){ out.textContent = "Need a>0, k≥0, n>1."; return; }
    const bits = k.toString(2);
    let r = 1;
    const lines = [`bits of k = ${bits}`, `start: r=1`];
    for(let i = 0; i < bits.length; i++){
      r = (r * r) % n;
      let line = `bit ${i} = ${bits[i]}: square → r=${r}`;
      if(bits[i] === "1"){
        r = (r * a) % n;
        line += `, multiply by ${a} → r=${r}`;
      }
      lines.push(line);
    }
    lines.push(`result: ${a}^${k} ≡ ${r} (mod ${n})`);
    out.textContent = lines.join("\n");
  }
  goBtn.addEventListener("click", run);
  run();
})();

/* ==== Schoof CRT recovery ==== */
(function(){
  const pIn = $("#sch-p"), tIn = $("#sch-t"), out = $("#sch-out"), svg = $("#sch-svg");
  const label = $("#sch-primes-label");
  const reset = $("#sch-reset");
  if(!svg) return;

  let primes = new Set([2, 3, 5]);
  const allPrimes = [2, 3, 5, 7];

  function setBtn(id, prime){
    const btn = $(id);
    if(!btn) return;
    function refresh(){ btn.classList.toggle("active", primes.has(prime)); }
    btn.addEventListener("click", () => {
      if(primes.has(prime)) primes.delete(prime); else primes.add(prime);
      refresh();
      redraw();
    });
    refresh();
  }
  setBtn("#sch-3", 3);
  setBtn("#sch-5", 5);
  setBtn("#sch-7", 7);
  reset.addEventListener("click", () => {
    primes = new Set([2, 3, 5]);
    ["#sch-3", "#sch-5", "#sch-7"].forEach((id, i) => {
      const p = [3, 5, 7][i];
      $(id).classList.toggle("active", primes.has(p));
    });
    redraw();
  });

  function redraw(){
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    const p = parseInt(pIn.value, 10);
    const t = parseInt(tIn.value, 10);
    if(!(p > 1)) return;
    const bound = 2 * Math.sqrt(p);
    const lo = Math.ceil(-bound), hi = Math.floor(bound);
    const W = 760, H = 200;
    const xMin = 40, xMax = 720;
    const span = hi - lo;
    const xOf = v => xMin + ((v - lo) / span) * (xMax - xMin);

    // axis
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line", {x1: xMin, y1: 100, x2: xMax, y2: 100, stroke: "var(--mute)"}));
    [lo, 0, hi].forEach(v => {
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line", {x1: xOf(v), y1: 95, x2: xOf(v), y2: 105, stroke: "var(--mute)"}));
      const lab = SVG("text", {x: xOf(v), y: 124, "text-anchor": "middle", "font-size": 11, fill: "var(--mute)"});
      lab.textContent = v;
      svg.appendChild(lab);
    });
    const haseLab = SVG("text", {x: 380, y: 28, "text-anchor": "middle", "font-size": 12, fill: "var(--mute)"});
    haseLab.textContent = `Hasse window: |t| ≤ 2√${p} ≈ ${bound.toFixed(2)}`;
    svg.appendChild(haseLab);

    // mark candidates that survive
    const tMod = (a, m) => ((a % m) + m) % m;
    const candidates = [];
    for(let v = lo; v <= hi; v++){
      let ok = true;
      for(const q of primes){
        if(tMod(v, q) !== tMod(t, q)){ ok = false; break; }
      }
      if(ok) candidates.push(v);
    }

    for(let v = lo; v <= hi; v++){
      const survives = candidates.includes(v);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle", {cx: xOf(v), cy: 100, r: survives ? 5 : 2, fill: survives ? "var(--cyan)" : "var(--line)", stroke: v === t ? "var(--green)" : "none", "stroke-width": 2}));
    }

    const tMark = SVG("text", {x: xOf(t), y: 76, "text-anchor": "middle", "font-size": 12, "font-weight": 600, fill: "var(--green)"});
    tMark.textContent = `t=${t}`;
    svg.appendChild(tMark);

    const list = [...primes].sort((a,b)=>a-b);
    label.textContent = list.join(",") || "(none)";
    out.textContent = `Used primes ℓ ∈ {${list.join(",")}}: ${candidates.length} candidate(s) remain in Hasse window. Product = ${list.reduce((a,b)=>a*b,1)} (need > ${(4*Math.sqrt(p)).toFixed(2)} for unique recovery).`;
  }

  pIn.addEventListener("input", redraw);
  tIn.addEventListener("input", redraw);
  redraw();
})();

/* ==== Buchmann factor base + SNF ==== */
(function(){
  const out = $("#bu-out"), svg = $("#bu-svg");
  const addBtn = $("#bu-add"), redBtn = $("#bu-reduce"), resetBtn = $("#bu-reset");
  if(!svg) return;

  // Factor base for Q(sqrt(-5)): mathfrak{p}_2, mathfrak{p}_3, mathfrak{p}_3'
  // Known relations: (2) = p2^2; (3) = p3 p3'; (1+sqrt(-5)) = p2 p3; (1-sqrt(-5)) = p2 p3'
  const cols = ["𝔭₂", "𝔭₃", "𝔭₃'"];
  const lib = [
    {gen: "(2)", row: [2, 0, 0]},
    {gen: "(3)", row: [0, 1, 1]},
    {gen: "(1+√−5)", row: [1, 1, 0]},
    {gen: "(1−√−5)", row: [1, 0, 1]}
  ];
  let used = [];
  let snf = null;

  function reset(){
    used = []; snf = null; redraw("Empty matrix — click 'add a relation' to populate.");
  }

  function addOne(){
    if(used.length >= lib.length){ out.textContent = "All four sample relations loaded. Compute SNF."; return; }
    used.push(lib[used.length]);
    snf = null;
    redraw(`Added ${lib[used.length-1].gen}. Matrix has ${used.length} row(s).`);
  }

  function smithNormalForm2x3(M){
    // ad-hoc SNF for our small concrete case; returns invariant factors.
    if(M.length === 0) return [];
    if(M.length === 1) return [Math.abs(gcdRow(M[0]))];
    // copy
    const A = M.map(r => r.slice());
    const r = A.length, c = A[0].length;
    function gcdRow(row){ return row.reduce((a,b)=>gcd(a,b), 0); }
    function gcd(a,b){ a=Math.abs(a); b=Math.abs(b); while(b){[a,b]=[b,a%b];} return a; }
    // Determinant of any r-subset of cols, gcd over subsets gives largest invariant factor product
    function detSquare(rows, picked){
      if(picked.length === 1) return rows[0][picked[0]];
      // 2x2
      const [c0, c1] = picked;
      return rows[0][c0]*rows[1][c1] - rows[0][c1]*rows[1][c0];
    }
    let dets = [];
    if(r === 2 && c === 3){
      dets = [detSquare(A, [0,1]), detSquare(A, [0,2]), detSquare(A, [1,2])];
    } else if(r === 3 && c === 3){
      function det3(mat){
        return mat[0][0]*(mat[1][1]*mat[2][2]-mat[1][2]*mat[2][1])
             - mat[0][1]*(mat[1][0]*mat[2][2]-mat[1][2]*mat[2][0])
             + mat[0][2]*(mat[1][0]*mat[2][1]-mat[1][1]*mat[2][0]);
      }
      dets = [det3(A)];
    } else if(r === 4 && c === 3){
      // 4 rows, 3 cols: minors of size 3 (pick 3 rows out of 4)
      function det3(mat){
        return mat[0][0]*(mat[1][1]*mat[2][2]-mat[1][2]*mat[2][1])
             - mat[0][1]*(mat[1][0]*mat[2][2]-mat[1][2]*mat[2][0])
             + mat[0][2]*(mat[1][0]*mat[2][1]-mat[1][1]*mat[2][0]);
      }
      const subsets = [[0,1,2],[0,1,3],[0,2,3],[1,2,3]];
      dets = subsets.map(s => det3([A[s[0]], A[s[1]], A[s[2]]]));
    }
    const d_top = dets.reduce((a,b)=>gcd(a,b), 0);
    // d_1: gcd of all entries
    const d_1 = A.flat().reduce((a,b)=>gcd(a,b), 0);
    // For r=2: invariants are d_1, d_top/d_1
    if(r === 2) return [d_1, d_top / d_1].filter(x => x !== 0);
    // For r >= 3: gcd of 2x2 minors gives d_2
    function det2(mat, rows, cs){
      return mat[rows[0]][cs[0]]*mat[rows[1]][cs[1]] - mat[rows[0]][cs[1]]*mat[rows[1]][cs[0]];
    }
    const minors2 = [];
    for(let i = 0; i < r; i++) for(let j = i+1; j < r; j++)
      for(let p = 0; p < c; p++) for(let q = p+1; q < c; q++)
        minors2.push(det2(A, [i,j], [p,q]));
    const d_2 = minors2.reduce((a,b)=>gcd(a,b), 0);
    if(c === 3 && r >= 3){
      return [d_1, d_2/d_1, d_top/d_2].filter(x => x !== 0);
    }
    return [d_1];
  }

  function classGroupFromInvariants(inv){
    // class group = Z^c / col(M^T) — invariant factors of the cokernel
    // For c columns and invariants d_1 | d_2 | ... | d_k (k = rank), the cokernel is
    // Z^(c-k) ⊕ ⊕ Z/d_i Z. Drop d_i = 1.
    const c = 3;
    const k = inv.length;
    const torsion = inv.filter(d => d !== 1).map(d => `ℤ/${d}ℤ`);
    const free = c - k;
    const parts = [];
    for(let i = 0; i < free; i++) parts.push("ℤ");
    parts.push(...torsion);
    if(parts.length === 0) parts.push("0");
    return parts.join(" ⊕ ");
  }

  function redraw(msg){
    svg.innerHTML = "";
    const W = 760, H = 240;
    const cellW = 70, cellH = 32;
    const startX = 100, startY = 60;

    // header
    cols.forEach((c, j) => {
      const t = SVG("text", {x: startX + j * cellW + cellW/2, y: startY - 10, "text-anchor": "middle", "font-size": 13, fill: "var(--cyan)", "font-weight": 600});
      t.textContent = c;
      svg.appendChild(t);
    });

    used.forEach((rel, i) => {
      const yi = startY + i * cellH;
      const lab = SVG("text", {x: startX - 12, y: yi + cellH/2 + 4, "text-anchor": "end", "font-size": 12, fill: "var(--mute)"});
      lab.textContent = rel.gen;
      svg.appendChild(lab);
      rel.row.forEach((v, j) => {
        const xj = startX + j * cellW;
        svg.appendChild(SVG("rect", {x: xj, y: yi, width: cellW, height: cellH, fill: v === 0 ? "var(--panel2)" : "var(--panel)", stroke: "var(--line)"}));
        const t = SVG("text", {x: xj + cellW/2, y: yi + cellH/2 + 5, "text-anchor": "middle", "font-size": 14, fill: v === 0 ? "var(--mute)" : "#fff"});
        t.textContent = v;
        svg.appendChild(t);
      });
    });

    if(used.length === 0){
      const t = SVG("text", {x: W/2, y: H/2, "text-anchor": "middle", "font-size": 14, fill: "var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = "(empty relation matrix)";
      svg.appendChild(t);
    }

    if(snf){
      const tx = SVG("text", {x: 480, y: 80, "font-size": 13, fill: "var(--yellow)", "font-weight": 600});
      tx.textContent = `invariant factors: ${snf.join(", ")}`;
      svg.appendChild(tx);
      const cg = SVG("text", {x: 480, y: 110, "font-size": 14, fill: "var(--green)", "font-weight": 700});
      cg.textContent = `Cl(K) ≅ ${classGroupFromInvariants(snf)}`;
      svg.appendChild(cg);
      const note = SVG("text", {x: 480, y: 140, "font-size": 11, fill: "var(--mute)"});
      note.textContent = "(true class group is ℤ/2ℤ)";
      svg.appendChild(note);
    }

    out.textContent = msg;
  }

  addBtn.addEventListener("click", addOne);
  redBtn.addEventListener("click", () => {
    if(used.length === 0){ out.textContent = "Add some relations first."; return; }
    const M = used.map(r => r.row);
    snf = smithNormalForm2x3(M);
    redraw(`SNF computed: ${snf.join(", ")}.`);
  });
  resetBtn.addEventListener("click", reset);
  reset();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* sidebar TOC builder + active-section highlighting */
(function(){
  function build(){
    const aside = document.querySelector('aside.sidetoc');
    const topnav = document.querySelector('nav.toc');
    if(!aside || !topnav) return;
    const srcs = [...topnav.querySelectorAll('a[href^="#"]')];
    if(!srcs.length) return;
    const title = document.createElement('div');
    title.className = 'sidetoc-title';
    title.textContent = 'On this page';
    aside.appendChild(title);
    const ol = document.createElement('ol');
    srcs.forEach(src => {
      const li = document.createElement('li');
      const a = document.createElement('a');
      a.href = src.getAttribute('href');
      a.textContent = src.textContent.replace(/\u00a0/g,' ').trim();
      li.appendChild(a); ol.appendChild(li);
    });
    aside.appendChild(ol);
    const links = [...aside.querySelectorAll('a')];
    const sections = [];
    links.forEach(a => {
      const id = a.getAttribute('href').slice(1);
      const s = document.getElementById(id);
      if(s) sections.push({a, s});
    });
    if(!sections.length) return;
    function update(){
      const threshold = 140;
      let activeLink = sections[0].a;
      for(const {a,s} of sections){
        if(s.getBoundingClientRect().top <= threshold) activeLink = a;
      }
      links.forEach(x => x.classList.remove('active'));
      activeLink.classList.add('active');
    }
    update();
    window.addEventListener('scroll', update, {passive:true});
    window.addEventListener('resize', update);
  }
  if(document.readyState === 'loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', build);
  else build();
})();
</script>
